assertion_results.text: Impact map or task descriptions explicitly flag at least 3 ambiguities from the feature description
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map (impact-map.md) explicitly flags 5 ambiguities: (1) 'Search should be faster' — no performance targets, (2) 'Results should be more relevant' — no relevance criteria, (3) 'Add filters' — no filter specification, (4) 'Should be fast enough' — non-functional requirement lacks quantification, (5) 'Don't break existing functionality' — no regression baseline. Each is documented with a labeled assumption pending clarification. This exceeds the minimum of 3.

assertion_results.text: Plan acknowledges that 'Better UI' (non-MVP) cannot be planned without design mockups or frontend repository and excludes it from scope
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map contains an 'Exclusion: Better UI — Out of scope' section (lines 68-76) that states: 'No design mockups or Figma designs are provided', 'No frontend repository is in scope (only trustify-backend is available)', 'UI improvements require design input and a frontend codebase', and 'This requirement is excluded from the current plan. It should be revisited when design mockups are available and a frontend repository is identified.' No tasks are created for Better UI.

assertion_results.text: Tasks still follow the task-description-template.md format despite ambiguous requirements
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks follow the template format with sections: Repository, Target Branch, Description, Files to Modify (and Files to Create where applicable), Implementation Notes, Acceptance Criteria, Test Requirements, Verification Commands, and Dependencies. Optional sections (API Changes, Reuse Candidates) are included where applicable and omitted where not, per template rules.

assertion_results.text: Tasks document assumptions where they fill in missing details, labeled as assumptions pending clarification
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain explicitly labeled assumptions: Task 1 (line 14): '**Assumption (pending clarification):** The specific columns to index are based on the existing entity model...'; Task 2 (line 14): '**Assumption (pending clarification):** Without specific performance metrics...'; Task 3 (line 14): '**Assumption (pending clarification):** Relevance scoring will use field-weight boosting...'; Task 4 (line 15): '**Assumption (pending clarification):** The following filters are implemented based on fields available in the existing entity model...'

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Repository section with a single repository name (not multiple repos per task)
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have '## Repository' followed by a single value 'trustify-backend': Task 1 (lines 1-2), Task 2 (lines 1-2), Task 3 (lines 1-2), Task 4 (lines 1-2). No task lists multiple repositories.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains Target Branch, Description, Acceptance Criteria, and Test Requirements sections as required by the handoff contract in task-description-template.md
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks contain all required sections: Task 1 has Target Branch (line 4), Description (line 7), Acceptance Criteria (line 49), Test Requirements (line 55). Task 2 has Target Branch (line 4), Description (line 7), Acceptance Criteria (line 53), Test Requirements (line 60). Task 3 has Target Branch (line 4), Description (line 7), Acceptance Criteria (line 67), Test Requirements (line 74). Task 4 has Target Branch (line 4), Description (line 7), Acceptance Criteria (line 68), Test Requirements (line 77).

assertion_results.text: File paths in Files to Modify and Files to Create reference paths from the repo-backend.md mock repository structure manifest, not invented paths
assertion_results.evidence: All Files to Modify paths exist in repo-backend.md: migration/src/lib.rs (line 17), modules/search/src/service/mod.rs (line 91), modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs (line 93). Files to Create follow established patterns: migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs follows the m0001_initial pattern (line 19-20), modules/search/src/model/mod.rs and search_result.rs follow the model directory pattern from modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/ (lines 40-43). No invented or implausible paths found.

assertion_results.text: Implementation Notes in every task reference specific file paths and code patterns from the repository, not abstract or generic guidance
assertion_results.evidence: Task 1 references: migration/src/m0001_initial/mod.rs, modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, entity/src/sbom.rs, entity/src/advisory.rs, entity/src/package.rs, and SeaORM patterns. Task 2 references: modules/search/src/service/mod.rs, common/src/db/query.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs, common/src/db/limiter.rs, common/src/error.rs. Task 3 references: modules/fundamental/src/sbom/model/summary.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, package/model/summary.rs, modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs, common/src/model/paginated.rs. Task 4 references: common/src/db/query.rs, modules/fundamental/src/sbom/endpoints/list.rs, advisory/endpoints/list.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs. All paths correspond to files in repo-backend.md.

assertion_results.text: Every generated task description contains a Target Branch section set to 'main'
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have '## Target Branch' followed by 'main': Task 1 (lines 4-5), Task 2 (lines 4-5), Task 3 (lines 4-5), Task 4 (lines 4-5).

assertion_results.text: After each task is created, a description digest comment is posted with a format-tagged SHA-256 hash — exactly 64 lowercase hex characters prefixed by 'sha256-md:' or 'sha256-adf:', not a placeholder, abbreviated value, or example string. Marker format: '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:<64-char-hex>' (or sha256-adf). The digest is computed by re-fetching the description from the API and running scripts/sha256-digest.py
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks have properly formatted digest comments: Task 1 (line 73): '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest: sha256-md:a2b3e89982ca7a9edebe736b8502714bad18c696b290ee35b3cea6d839af8398' (64 hex chars verified). Task 2 (line 76): 'sha256-md:3a6aa1435aa7891a0967aee2e400e2a9b79dcfce69a48ec2f151534288c6f92f' (64 hex chars). Task 3 (line 91): 'sha256-md:3ff60daa85bc96fd5b592f8569c6da09019eb7c42faeb5d0867ec5c691c30009' (64 hex chars). Task 4 (line 98): 'sha256-md:c4d80c1a22385843495fcc57e75430d48cab61bad7aebf8249726d68d1ba5c14' (64 hex chars). All use sha256-md: prefix, all are 64 lowercase hex characters, none are placeholders.

assertion_results.text: Convention-aware enrichment validates file-type applicability per shared/convention-applicability-rules.md before including a convention — inapplicable conventions are excluded entirely (not listed with 'Not applicable' annotations), and applicable ones include a rationale in the prescribed format ('Applies: task modifies <file> matching the convention's <scope>'), not free-form prose
assertion_results.evidence: All convention rationales follow the prescribed format. Task 1: 'Applies: task creates `migration/src/m0002_search_indexes/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust migration file scope.' Task 2: 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/service/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust service file scope.' and 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/service/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust source file scope.' Task 3: 'Applies: task creates `modules/search/src/model/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust module structure scope.' and 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/service/mod.rs` matching the convention's Rust service file scope.' Task 4: three conventions all with 'Applies: task modifies `modules/search/src/endpoints/mod.rs` matching the convention's ...' format. No inapplicable conventions are listed with 'Not applicable' annotations. No free-form prose rationales found.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a priority set (not 'Undefined'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'priority' with the inherited priority name. When the Feature's priority is 'Undefined', the priority key is omitted entirely from additional_fields (not set to null or 'Undefined')
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks include priority in additional_fields: Task 1 (line 68): 'priority: Normal', Task 2 (line 71): 'priority: Normal', Task 3 (line 86): 'priority: Normal', Task 4 (line 93): 'priority: Normal'. The feature priority was 'Normal' (not 'Undefined'), and all tasks inherit it consistently.

assertion_results.text: When the Feature issue has a non-empty fixVersions array and the fixVersion scope config (from ### Jira Field Defaults in CLAUDE.md) is 'task' or 'both' (or absent, defaulting to 'both'), every created task's additional_fields includes 'fixVersions' with the inherited version(s). When fixVersion scope is 'feature' or the Feature has no fixVersions, the fixVersions key is omitted entirely from additional_fields
assertion_results.evidence: All 4 tasks include fixVersions in additional_fields: Task 1 (line 69): 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.6.0"]', Task 2 (line 72): 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.6.0"]', Task 3 (line 87): 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.6.0"]', Task 4 (line 94): 'fixVersions: ["RHTPA 1.6.0"]'. The CLAUDE.md has no '### Jira Field Defaults' section, so fixVersion scope defaults to 'both', and the Feature had fixVersions set to ['RHTPA 1.6.0'], so tasks correctly inherit them.

assertion_results.text: The summary comment on the feature issue (Step 6c) includes the inherited priority and fixVersion values that were propagated to tasks, or states they were omitted and why
assertion_results.evidence: The impact map (impact-map.md) does not contain any mention of inherited priority or fixVersion values. It covers ambiguities (lines 3-76), workflow mode (lines 78-89), and impact map structure (lines 91-103), but has no summary comment documenting field inheritance. No other output file contains a summary comment about propagated priority ('Normal') or fixVersion ('RHTPA 1.6.0') values. The only evidence of these fields is in each task's additional_fields section, but no summary comment documents what was propagated.
